name: Slack Messages
description: Slack message bodies for each conversation included in slack.conversations, with thread replies attached inline. Optionally limited by a history-depth window.

teamId.description: Slack team id this message export belongs to.

oldestTs.description: Slack-format unix timestamp (seconds, as string) cutoff used for this run. Messages older than this were not fetched. Null means all-time.

oldestDays.description: History-depth window in days that produced oldestTs. Null means all-time.

fetchThreads.description: Whether thread replies were collected via conversations.replies for messages with reply_count > 0.

conversations.description: Per-conversation message buckets. Each entry corresponds to a slack.conversations entry whose skipReason is null.

name.description: Human-readable conversation name (channel name or peer display name for DMs).

messageCount.description: Number of parent messages collected for this conversation.

messages.description: Slack message objects as returned by conversations.history, with optional 'thread_replies' attached for thread parents.

ts.description: Slack message timestamp; unique per conversation.

user.description: Slack user id of the message author. May be null for system messages.

text.description: Message text with Slack markup (user mentions, channel refs, links).

thread_ts.description: Slack ts of the parent message when this message is inside a thread. Equal to ts on parents that have replies.

subtype.description: Slack subtype (e.g. 'channel_join', 'bot_message'). Absent on plain user messages.

reply_count.description: Slack-reported reply count for thread parents.

blocks.description: Slack rich-text 'blocks' representation of the message body, when present.

thread_replies.description: Thread replies collected via conversations.replies, excluding the parent message. Present only when the parent had reply_count > 0 and the fetch succeeded.

thread_error.description: Slack error returned when conversations.replies failed for this thread parent.

fetchError.description: Error returned by conversations.history when the conversation could not be fetched (e.g. not_in_channel, missing_scope).

totalMessages.description: Total parent messages collected across all conversations.

totalThreads.description: Number of thread parents for which replies were fetched.
